task_completion.description: The reference image sequence is imported into the animation software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Xsheet or Timeline shows a level loaded with the 'bounce' reference frames, or the bouncing ball reference image is visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: A visible outline tracing the ball is created on Frame 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the timeline playhead on frame 1, a newly drawn stroke or outline is visible around or on top of the reference ball in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: A visible outline tracing the ball is created on Frame 12.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the timeline playhead on frame 12, a newly drawn stroke or outline is visible around or on top of the reference ball in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: A visible outline tracing the ball is created on Frame 24.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the timeline playhead on frame 24, a newly drawn stroke or outline is visible around or on top of the reference ball in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: The render output settings are configured to export a PNG sequence to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open, showing the save path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/rotoscope_test/' and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: The scene is rendered, exporting the combined reference and trace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar/window is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created PNG files in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/rotoscope_test/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the OpenToonz interface (menus, timeline, drawing tools) rather than exclusively using terminal commands to generate or move files.

integrity.description: The agent manually traced the ball using drawing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen selecting and using brush/pencil tools in the OpenToonz toolbar to draw the outlines, rather than running an image processing script.

integrity.description: The agent used the software's render function to produce the final output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Render' in the OpenToonz menu or Output Settings, rather than copying the reference files directly to the output directory via terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The reference image sequence is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/bounce_ref' and contains exactly 24 frames. The agent must trace the ball on at least frames 1, 12, and 24. The final output must be rendered as a PNG sequence to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/rotoscope_test/'. The rendered output must include both the original reference background and the newly drawn traced strokes.
